commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# rarcrack --help
 RarCrack! 0.2 by David Zoltan Kedves (kedazo@gmail.com)
 
 Usage:   rarcrack encrypted_archive.ext [--threads NUM] [--type rar|zip|7z]
 
 Options: --help: show this screen.
        --type: you can specify the archive program, this needed when
                the program couldn't detect the proper file type
        --threads: you can specify how many threads
                   will be run (default: 1)
 
 Info:    This program supports only RAR, ZIP and 7Z encrypted archives.
        RarCrack! usually detects the archive type.

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=71bc9c405feeb2234a9ace9587271a0c51098a86, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Password cracker for rar archives
This program uses a brute force algorithm to guess your encrypted compressed
file's password.
.
This program can crack zip, 7z, and rar file passwords.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use rarcrack because ### rarcrack.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `rarcrack encrypted_archive.ext [--threads NUM] [--type rar|zip|7z]` as documented upstream for rarcrack.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair rarcrack with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `rarcrack --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install rarcrack. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `rarcrack encrypted_archive.ext [--threads NUM] [--type rar|zip|7z]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### rarcrack.
